activities_projects.title: Belgium vs Sri Lanka Comparison
activities_projects.objective: Analyse how two countries handled ethnic diversity
steps: Create a two-column table for Belgium and Sri Lanka
List population groups and languages spoken
Explain how each country addressed power-sharing issues
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands contrasting approaches to managing diversity
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy and clarity of comparison (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Ethnic Conflict Timeline
activities_projects.objective: Understand historical developments leading to conflicts
steps: Research key events in Sri Lanka from independence (1948) to the civil war
List major policies affecting Tamil communities
Create a timeline chart showing major developments
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops chronological understanding of political events

activities_projects.title: Majoritarianism Case Analysis
steps: Define majoritarianism
Identify policies that favoured the Sinhala-speaking majority
Explain how these policies affected minority groups
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognises the political consequences of exclusion

activities_projects.title: Belgian Federal System Diagram
steps: Draw a diagram showing Central Government, State Governments and Community Governments
Label responsibilities of each level
Explain how this arrangement maintains balance

activities_projects.title: Prudential vs Moral Reasons Debate
activities_projects.objective: Differentiate between two types of reasons for power sharing
steps: Divide the class into two groups
One group explains prudential reasons
Another group explains moral reasons with examples
Summarise conclusions from the debate

activities_projects.title: Forms of Power Sharing Research
activities_projects.objective: Identify real-world examples of power sharing
steps: List the four forms of power sharing
Find one real-life example of each form
Explain how these arrangements work
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects theory with real political systems

activities_projects.title: Coalition Government Simulation
activities_projects.objective: Understand power sharing among political parties
steps: Assign students different political parties
Conduct a mock election where no party gets majority
Negotiate and form a coalition government

activities_projects.title: Power Sharing in India Study
activities_projects.objective: Examine how India practices power sharing
steps: Identify different levels of government in India
Explain how powers are divided between Union and States
Give examples of reserved constituencies
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands federal and social power sharing in India

activities_projects.title: News Case Study on Conflict
activities_projects.objective: Analyse modern conflicts related to power sharing
steps: Find a news article about a political or ethnic conflict
Identify groups involved in the conflict
Suggest how power sharing could help resolve it
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies theoretical concepts to current events

activities_projects.title: Community Representation Survey
activities_projects.objective: Observe representation of different groups in governance
steps: Research elected representatives in your local government
Identify representation of different communities
Write a short report on diversity in leadership
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of research and reporting (10 marks)
